summary: CRITICAL VIOLATION: Task explicitly requires 'Do NOT test your script directly — all testing goes through the pipeline,' but tests/cleanup_artifacts.bats runs ~15 unit tests directly against the script, not through `act`. The required architecture is: bats tests invoke act, which runs the workflow. Instead, this suite has direct unit tests + separate act-based integration tests. Fixture files (fixtures/case*.tsv) are referenced in workflow.bats but not provided. Coverage: all main policies tested individually (max-age, keep-latest, max-total-size, dry-run, combined), but missing edge cases (identical timestamps, boundary conditions, zero-size artifacts, no-policies scenario). Rigor: happy path + basic error handling only; no boundary value testing. Design: good local organization (setup/teardown, clear names, proper isolation), but fundamental architectural flaw violates core requirement. act-result.txt would be generated by workflow.bats, but cleanup_artifacts.bats output would not be captured there, creating two separate test streams. The suite would catch obvious regressions but would fail to validate the required end-to-end pipeline behavior specified in the task.
